codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that fulfills all three requirements: (1) asset panel loads project media from 4 Supabase tables with deduplication, (2) assets are draggable onto timeline tracks at drop position using the existing custom DnD system, (3) studio-generated and project-setup assets are discoverable. Code is well-structured with proper error handling (graceful partial results), good test coverage (12 new tests across 2 files), and follows existing project conventions. Minor cosmetic and style issues noted below.

issues.description: Prompt truncation for generation_outputs always appends '...' even for short prompts. `record.prompt?.slice(0, 40) ? record.prompt.slice(0, 37) + '...'` will turn a 5-char prompt 'Hello' into 'Hello...'. Should conditionally add ellipsis only when prompt length > 37.

All four Supabase table queries use `(supabase as any)` casts, adding 4 new `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` violations (acknowledged in handoff as matching existing pattern). These tables likely aren't in the generated Supabase types yet. Acceptable for now, but should be tracked for future type generation update.

`clearMediaState` is defined as a standalone function outside the component, directly calling `useVideoEditorStore.setState()`. This works but is non-standard — a store action would be more maintainable and discoverable. The store already has `clearMediaLibrary` action which could be extended.

The effect dependency array `[params.projectId, project.id, setProjectId, setProjectName]` is correct but the effect calls `store.loadProject()` and `store.loadMediaLibrary()` which are not in the dep array. Since these are accessed via `getState()` inside the effect, this is technically fine, but worth noting for future maintainers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used `(supabase as any)` for 4 table queries because the Supabase generated types don't include media_items, project_assets, generation_outputs, or final_project_assets tables. This pattern is used elsewhere in the codebase but AGENTS.md doesn't mention it as an expected pattern or document which tables are missing from the schema types.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/videoEditorService.ts:31-48 — all four .from() calls use (supabase as any). Comment at line 15 says 'Database tables timeline_clips and compositions don't exist yet in the schema'. The generated types at src/integrations/supabase/types.ts likely don't include these tables.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure correctly: planned implementation, wrote tests, implemented backend-first then frontend, ran all validators. No deviations reported and skillFeedback confirms procedure was followed.

summary: Feature passes review. The implementation correctly fulfills all three requirements: (1) ProjectAssetsTab with search/filter loads assets from 4 Supabase tables via videoEditorService.getMediaLibrary(), (2) MediaItem components use the existing custom DnD system (useDrag/useDrop) enabling drag-to-timeline at computed drop position, (3) project_assets (Studio outputs), generation_outputs (AI results), and final_project_assets (project-setup shots) are all queried and merged with URL-based deduplication. 12 new tests pass covering service aggregation, dedup, error handling, and UI states. TypeScript compiles cleanly. Non-blocking issues are cosmetic (prompt truncation) and style-related (any casts, standalone function).
